- Service Account Name : [local-mysql-auth-proxy@ingka-ca-family-prod.iam.gserviceaccount.com|mailto:local-mysql-auth-proxy@ingka-ca-family-prod.iam.gserviceaccount.com]
- Days to Expire : 14 
- *{color:#de350b}Date of Expiry: 2025-07-29 15:21:38+00:00{color}*

*Requestor:* Christopher Hawkins

*Description:*
Since approximately August 3rd or 4th, no sellback offers have shown as "notification sent." Previously, it was rare for notifications not to send, assuming customers had opted in. This issue affects *every sellback offer* received since that date.

*Impact:*
 * At least one customer reported not receiving a notification despite a reply being sent.
 * Potential for multiple customers to be unaware of responses, impacting experience and trust.

*Steps to Reproduce:*
 # Submit a sellback offer as a customer.
 # Respond to the offer from the system.
 # Check if notification status updates or if the customer receives the notification.

*Expected Result:*
Notification should be sent and logged as "notification sent."

*Actual Result:*
No notifications are sent or logged for any sellback offers since Aug 3–4.

*Date First Noticed:*
Around August 3–4, 2025.

summary: CtryDev - Data Assessment risk Mitigation management system
description:
||Question||Answer||
|Country requesting|[Canada]|
|Additional countries| |
|Description of Need (focus on WHAT and WHY)|Framework  to efficiently manage the Data Life Cycle, ensure Compliance, maintain Data Quality, evaluate Performance, and mitigate risks associated with data quality, data retention, and monitoring throughout the data's life cycle.|
|Business Case|Switching to a stand-alone application for data assessments will enhance organization, speed, and collaboration while cutting down on administrative costs and improving process efficiency. It will streamline our entire process, reducing errors and setting the foundation for scaling operations as our business grows all while keeping data secure and easy to manage.\\  \\ Efficiency: \\ Automates and centralizes workflows, saving up to 2 hours per assessment and minimizing errors. This results in approximately 100 hours saved per year for data assessments.\\  \\ Collaboration: \\ Creates a communication layer around each data assessment, improving team interaction and feedback.\\  \\ Data Integrity: \\ - Ensures version control and structured data management.\\ - Monitor data quality and life cycle \\ - Data Access control notifications\\ \\ Security: \\ Provides a secure, auditable environment for managing sensitive data.\\  \\ Scalability: \\ - Adapts to our evolving needs, offering flexibility and customization as our business expands.\\ - Provide standards across countries\\ \\ \\ Example:\\ The cleanup of postal code data resulted in savings of over $225,000 for the year. \\ However, we currently lack a system to effectively monitor data quality and identify issues that require action.\\ |
|Main Business Value Narrative|[Value Driver - Cost reduction, Value Driver - Productivity increase, Value Enabler - Foundational Improvement]|
|Business Value|Medium|
|Urgency|High|
|Business Stakeholder|Guido DiCesare|
|Owning Business Function| |
|Digital Stakeholder|Guido DiCesare, Romanios Zakhour, Wojciech Matwiejczyk|
|System/Product| |
|Digital Domain| |
|Product Area| |
|Central or Country development|Country Development (standalone)|
|General comment| |

Per email from [~khria]:

Hej,

 

After completing the learning step, we are ready to move to the next one, the gap assessment.

The goal of this work package is to map all local services/digital solutions and/or products/hardware that are impacted by doing a risk assessment and then create a plan with next steps.

 

Please see below the steps suggested by the Global launch team:
 # Meet with Legal representative to identify scope
 # Do a risk assessment with use of BR&C template and methodology
 # Country Digital to create a plan with a timeline

 

 *++* 

*+Action:+* Please review the [Work package #5 Country gap assessed, scope and risks agreed|https://confluence.build.ingka.ikea.com/spaces/GP/pages/791890894/Work+package+5+Country+gap+assessed+scope+and+risks+agreed], as well as the [Z Supporting documents & links|https://confluence.build.ingka.ikea.com/spaces/GP/pages/791890917/Z+Supporting+documents+links]

 

Other documents to use: [Local solutions list template|https://iweof.sharepoint.com/:x:/t/o365g_digitalideainternal_itnl012/EZ0q3EP5I3FMtIjPl44IAtgByjr0i8MKc-mSsP_hZlF6Xg?e=SqGdAT&%3d%3d&sdata=NVo5OGgyWWRVa0dZTkUwYkh1czRla1dMRVZyNFdTT3cvM2VUaE5BSFROTT0%3d] ({*}Copied for CA{*}{*}: see link in comments{*}) and [Risk Workshop template version 1.0|https://iweof.sharepoint.com/:p:/t/o365g_digitalideainternal_itnl012/EZK__XSrHwxGvVUlLyFUxpsBYE8J3Dk93HyfLTVhRFYLDw?e=bEMCgL&%3d%3d&sdata=N0luK2lvNHdNUStYSk04ZDEyS01FQy9hUnEzWWk0OGNOcW13eWNlWCszVT0%3d]

 

 

_N.B: A meeting will be scheduled to go over the risk assessment._

summary: All activities for Afterpay in CA
description:
* Pay-in-4 payment solution
 * Digital payment platform offered to online shoppers that allows them to delay payments on purchases. Customers pay 25% of the purchase upfront and then 25% in 3 bi-weekly payments until they are paid in full. No credit check is required to use AfterPay, and no interest is charged.
 * Currently available for online purchases online. In-store is currently planned to be completed in T1
 * For purchases between $50-1000

[https://confluence.build.ingka.ikea.com/display/CANADAHUB/Afterpay+in+CA]

summary: DROMSACK - Power App integration with CDC for inbound audits and outbound shipments
description:
System Master Catalog Entry:
[https://jira.digital.ingka.com/secure/insight/assets/SMC-566129]


 # 
System Home
[https://apps.powerapps.com/play/7368f6bb-6eb7-42c1-9f67-03a0787a5b54?tenantId=720b637a-655a-40cf-816a-f22f40755c2c]
 # 
Business Description
Business Case

Save on labour costs by improving trailer receiving efficiency.
Save on hardware costs.
Capture damages, increase reporting to improve the Happy Customer Score.
Improve documentation of shipments.
Save space and streamline our workflow..
Get rid of some of the equipment our business currently uses.
Report the state of the trailers.
Spend less on paper and printing supplies.
Save time creating and organizing files.
Increase the security of our documentation and have a better access to useful data that can improve our operations.

Target Group:
CDC, Gatekeepers, Shipper / Receivers, Loaders / Unloaders

 # 
Technical Description
Application created in Power Apps that supports day to day operations.

summary: Update Variera Reciever Emails for Kitchen Contractor Form
Update Variera Payload to include emails

description:
*Description*

*Background*
Lucas Selby has identified the need for a new subcontractor web form to replace the existing Netsuite solution. We already have a reusable mechanism in place—our Form Handler—but we must submit an LNM to provision a dedicated form instance.

*Requirements*
 # Provision a new form via the Form Handler.

 # Fields and labels must match the current Netsuite form, with the following enhancements:

 ** Service Area: list of all markets

 ** RBQ license # (Québec)

*Next Steps*
 * @Wojciech Matwiejczyk to reach out to @Martin Roy for the detailed specifications required to complete the LNM.

 * Gather any additional field requirements or business rules from Martin.

 * Prepare and submit the LNM to the appropriate intake queue.

summary: This epic is for tasks related to visibility, communications efforts and ambassadorship for the Digital Products team.

description:
*Description:*
We need to manage the review and registration of the existing manager schedule display solution at the Coquitlam store. The current setup shows four weeks of manager schedules on a TV in-store. While this has been running for several years, recent ISDP concerns were raised about the risk of the computer being compromised.

*Scope and steps:*
 * Coordinate with Russell Jen to gather details on the current setup (hardware, software, network, access, location, data shown, etc.).

 * Share info with Ricardo Hernandez and Guido DiCesare to kick off ISDP and data assessments.

 * Provide Rory Young and Amardeep Roopah for visibility and oversight.

 * Submit an LNM request through Voice of the Countries (VOC) to formalize the need and request:

 ** Interim approval for the current solution, to avoid disruption

 ** Guidance/approval on next steps:

 *** Backfill as local solution in SMC (with changes as needed)

 *** Identify a global replacement, or

 *** Develop a new local solution (buy/build)

 * Track progress, document risks, and keep stakeholders updated in the ticket.

*Key contacts:*
 * Store: Russell Jen

 * ISDP: Ricardo Hernandez

 * Data: Guido DiCesare

 * Oversight: Rory Young, Amardeep Roopah

 * Process Owner: Wojciech Matwiejczyk (back-up: Charles Simard)

*Goal:*
Stay compliant with digital standards, avoid store disruption, and ensure a scalable solution going forward.
----

This application will integrate with Jira for creation and interaction with various Jira projects. Because some activities will be run by the application itself while unattended, an M2M API token is required. 

If a service account is not possible, we will need to consider creating a Jira user for this application and using that as an M2M token, which will require creating an email account for the application and registering it with JIRA.

[~womat1] to investigate if service account token is possible (including through escalation if required) or if we will resort to creating an application email account and JIRA PAT.
Because this application will generate emails as part of its functionality, we need to obtain SMTP credentials for sending them.

description:
* Find a way to send layout image in email.

Create new form field types:

BUCKET_REF_SVG
BUCKET_REF_JPEG
BUCKET_REF_PNG

accepts a string identifier, returns a bucket url, combining the form id and the string identifier that corresponds with a file in the bucket.

Example:
field_name: layout_id
field_type: corresponds with BUCKET_REF_JPEG
form_id: custom_countertop_039
field_value: 'layout_1'

payload:
{code:java}
{ form_type: 'custom_countertop_039', layout_id : 'layout_1' } {code}
builds url:

[https://www.bucket.com/custom_countertop_039/layout_1.jpg|https://www.bucket.com/custom_countertop_039/layout1.jpg]

summary: SELP (FRIZBEE solution) in CA for BULK Giftcards
description:
Form on ikea.com that integrates with the Frizbee platform (SELP is the company). 

Form captures IKEA business customers request for bulk gift card orders over $10000.

Information includes:

 - BCM id (IKEA Business Network partyUID)
 - Quantity and type of gift cards.
 - Amount of order.
 - Types of giftcards:
     = Email sending (digital)
     = Physical
     = Batches of codes (digital)

Upon submitting form Frizbee platform will return a TOTP key for customers to setup their Authenticator application, and an email will be sent to the following containing the order information:

Anna Furtado - IKEA Business Leader
Khadeejah Muto - IKEA Business Specialist
IKEA Business Remote CMP

Frizbee will access minimal Business Customer Information as needed transactionally via IKEA BCM to avoid their system storing PII.

- Email
- Full name
- Business Number 
- Business Name

The Frizbee platform will use a client Authenticator using TOTP key  to authenticate customer on their site thereafter and verify the account is still active using BCM.

If available BCM single sign-on will be preferable to TOTP. But TOTP can be implemented if SSO is not available.

Example table for users would contain an IKEA BCM ID, the corresponding PartyUID, and TOTP Seed for Authenticator:
 
|ID  |BCMID        |PartyUID     |TOTPSeed
|   1|2312434325   |\{UID}        |\{hash}
 
Example table for TOTP challenge codes would contain an auditable record of BCMID, temporary TOTP code, and its’ expiry date in UTC seconds:
 
|ID  |BCMID        |TOTPCode     |Expiry
|   1|2312434325   |91606        |1684350296286

Single Sign-on FLOW DIAGRAM attached. (OR TOTP)
 
Customer completes form on IKEA.COM
By pressing SUBMIT, the form data BCM partyUID is sent to Frizbee
FRIZBEE creates an account for this BCM customer if needed
FRIZBEE adds the transaction request for the account
FRIZBEE returns a success message in the API response and optional unique link or TOTP key for sign-in
The form page on IKEA.COM is updated to inform the customer that their order is sent and provided a sign-in link
The Customer clicks the sign-in link and goes to the FRIZBEE platform to sign in
Customer signs-in to FRIZBEE and manages their order(s)

# 
Processes payments taken over the phone via PCI-compliant payment processing.

 # 
Technical Description
Alliance is a system in which CWs transfer customers to a PCI-compliant third-party (Datatel) phone system where customers can then enter their credit card information. This is then sent to credit checks via Datatel’s system and a file is generated on their server with a ‘approved’ or ‘not approved’ status. A local script picks up these files.

 * For purchases between $50-1000

Business Description
PowerBI application that contains many dashboards containing various dynamic reports. Provides detailed data on KPIs business stakeholders need to make decisions. From top level stats such as happy customer score, all the way to coworker and customer senstive data such as interaction stats (handle time, etc.) and sales values and data enrichment (iSell, SäljaPro, SAMS), among others.

 # 
Technical Description
PowerBI application that contains many dashboards containing various dynamic reports.

System Master Catalog Entry:
[https://jira.digital.ingka.com/secure/insight/assets/SMC-136828]


 # 
System Home
[https://confluence.build.ingka.ikea.com/display/CANADAHUB/Data+Warehouse]
 # 
Business Description
Database holding info from disparate systems (e.g. sales, telephone stats, employees, etc.)

 # 
Technical Description
MySQL database hosted on a local server (RETCA230-NT8011, as of 2023-10-17) holding info from disparate systems (e.g. sales, telephone stats, employees, etc.)

 # 
Nickname Description
internal Data Warehouse

summary: Create script to upload / sync email templates.
description:
I would be easier to manage the email templates if there was a script to upload and replace the existing email templates. 

The email templates are currently in a google bucket.

form_handler_email_templates

They are also in the ca-form-handler-gcp github repo:
[https://github.com/ingka-group-digital/ca-form-handler-gcp/tree/main/email_templates]

The script would be an npm script that during deployment would upload the email templates if there are any to the google bucket. This would remove the manual process of having to do this.
